name: Matcha
summary: Stone-ground whole green-tea leaf — so you drink the entire leaf and get a bigger dose of the same catechins and L-theanine that make green tea worth drinking.
superfoodReason: Because you consume the powdered whole leaf rather than a steeped infusion, matcha delivers a higher concentration of catechins (EGCG) and L-theanine than regular green tea.

benefits.mechanism: Matcha is powdered whole green-tea leaf, so a serving carries more of the catechin EGCG than steeped tea. These catechins reduce cholesterol absorption and support blood-vessel function — the same pathway shown to lower LDL cholesterol in green-tea trials.

citations.title: Green tea intake lowers fasting serum total and LDL cholesterol in adults: a meta-analysis of 14 randomized controlled trials (Zheng et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2011)

benefits.mechanism: Matcha pairs caffeine with a high dose of L-theanine, which raises alpha brain-wave activity — together improving attention while smoothing out caffeine's jitteriness.

citations.title: The combined effects of L-theanine and caffeine on cognitive performance and mood (Owen et al., Nutr Neurosci 2008)

surprisingFacts.text: Because you ingest the whole ground leaf instead of discarding steeped leaves, matcha gives you several times the catechins of an equivalent cup of brewed green tea.

cautions.effect: You swallow the whole leaf rather than an infusion, so matcha carries more caffeine than the same volume of brewed green tea — jitteriness and poor sleep come on sooner. The tannins also blunt iron absorption when taken with meals.
cautions.threshold: Several bowls, or any close to bedtime.

citations.title: Matcha Green Tea: Chemical Composition, Phenolic Acids, Caffeine and Fatty Acid Profile (Foods 2024)
